RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues other than punitive damages and taken off calendar at to the issue of punitive damages because plaintiffs have dismissed their request for punitive damages. Ronald Edwards' declaration and defendant's General Order 129 interrogatory responses create a triable issue whether Louis Edwards was exposed to an asbestos-containing pre-mix topping compound attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Ronald Edwards' declaration are overruled. The only summary adjudication properly raised by defendant's statement was on the issue of punitive damages. Frank M. Stevens, Esq. of STEVENS, DRUMMOND & GIFFORD (925-944-5550), specially appearing for defendant / moving party Kelly-Moore Paint Co., Inc.
Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Theresa Edwards

RULING:
DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as plaintiffs have dismissed their claim for false representation and request for punitive damages. Defendant did not meet its burden of showing that plaintiffs knew or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known that the death of Louis Edwards was caused or contributed to by his exposure to asbestos. (C.C.P. 340.2(c)). Nor did defendant did satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Louis Edwards was exposed to asbestos-containing topping compound attributable to defendant because plaintiffs' interrogatory responses are not factually devoid on this issue. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
